MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-23-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. KATHRYN L. ANDERSON, TEL. NO. 901-0514 FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JANET L. EVERSON OF MURPHY, PEARSON & FEENEY, TEL. NO. 788-1900 FOR THE DEFENDANT ALEXANDER ANOLIK;

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT ALEXANDER ANOLIK, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED AS TO 1) NEGLIGENCE; 2) BREACH FIDUCIARY DUTY BECAUSE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT; DENIED FOR NON DISCLOSURE CLAIM BECAUSE ANOLIK IS NOT A PARTY TO THAT CLAIM; GRANTED AS TO 1) ATTORNEY'S FEE 2) EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DAMAGES; TO THE EXTENT THAT MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER AS TO THESE LATTER TWO ITEMS, COURT WILL TREAT MOTION AS MOTION TO STRIKE AND WILL STRIKE THEM. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. =(301/PJB/X-GW) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; REPORTER: CANDACE YOUNT, CSR 2737

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-17-2006 Continued To Jul-24-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel,
Off Calendar Jun-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Erik Weiss, for Defendant/Moving party Alexander Anolik
Kathryn L. Anderson, for Plaintiff/Opposing party
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modifications: Defendant Alexander Anolik's Motion To Compel is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as it seeks a prior mental examination report. It is not clear what additional information is being sought regarding alleged damages for emotional distress.
The motion is granted as it seeks to require responses to questions about prior fee disputes between Plaintiff and Mr. Anolik, including the nature of the emotional distress claimed at that time.
The motion is granted as it seeks the amount of the settlement of the damages claims with Kerr & Wagstaffe.

MODIFICATION:
NOT INCLUDING COMMUNICATIONS WITH MR. ANOLIK.
COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Russell S. Roeca, Esq. of ROECA HAAS HAGER, LLP 352-0980 for defendants RUTCHIK, RICHARD IDELL and IDELL, BERMAN, SEITEL & RUTCHIK, LLP petitioning to compel arbitration and stay further proceedings
Steven A. Kronenberg, Esq. of MURPHY, PEARSON, et al. for defendant ALEXANDER ANOLIK demurring and also moving to strike portios of Plaintiff's Amended Complaint
Kathryn L. Anderson, Esq. 901-0514 for Plaintiff and opposing the demurrer
1. Both attorneys Anderson and Kronenberg agreed to the Court's tentative ruling re the demurrer and the motion to strike with the following modification -- both parties agreed to strike Paragraphs 1 and 2. Tentative ruling adopted on the demurrer with the modification. Order signed in open court.
2. Petition to compel arbitration was argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling --
DEFENDANT GREGORY RUTCHIK, RICHARD IDELL, IDELL,BERMAN,SEITEL & RUTCHIK LLP Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings -- granted, although last sentence of arbitration agreement is deleted as substantively unconscionable. (CCP 1670.5(a) (301/JLW/ds) Judge James L. Warren, Reporter Christina Paxton, CSR #1558.
